questions.q: By what honorific title, meaning 'Great Leader', was Muhammad Ali Jinnah widely known?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was known as Quaid-e-Azam, meaning 'Great Leader'.

questions.q: Muhammad Ali Jinnah led which political organisation in the demand for Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was the leader of the All-India Muslim League.

questions.q: What profession did Jinnah practise before and during his political career?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was a barrister by profession.

questions.q: Jinnah became the first holder of which office in the new state of Pakistan in 1947?
questions.explanation: Jinnah became the first Governor-General of Pakistan in 1947.

questions.q: Early in his career Jinnah was hailed as an ambassador of what?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was once hailed as an ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity.

questions.q: Jinnah is most associated with championing the creation of which country?
questions.explanation: Jinnah championed the demand for Pakistan.

questions.q: In which year did Pakistan come into being with Jinnah as its first Governor-General?
questions.explanation: Pakistan was created in 1947, with Jinnah as its first Governor-General.

questions.q: Before fully committing to the League, Jinnah had also been a member of which other major nationalist body?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was for years a prominent member of the Indian National Congress before leaving it.

questions.q: Which 1940 resolution, demanding separate states for Muslims, did Jinnah preside over?
questions.explanation: Jinnah presided over the 1940 session that adopted the Lahore Resolution.

questions.q: Jinnah is generally regarded as the principal founder of which nation?
questions.explanation: Jinnah is regarded as the founder of Pakistan.

questions.q: In which city was Muhammad Ali Jinnah born in 1876?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was born in Karachi in 1876.

questions.q: Jinnah played a leading role in negotiating which 1916 agreement between the Congress and the Muslim League?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was a key architect of the 1916 Lucknow Pact between the Congress and the League.

questions.q: Jinnah famously left the Congress partly in disagreement with the methods of which leader's mass movements?
questions.explanation: Jinnah grew estranged from the Congress over Gandhi's mass agitational methods and left in the early 1920s.

questions.q: Jinnah's well-known set of constitutional demands put forward in 1929 are remembered as the what?
questions.explanation: Jinnah formulated his 'Fourteen Points' in 1929 as a charter of Muslim constitutional safeguards.

questions.q: In the late 1930s Jinnah spent a period of self-imposed political exile practising law in which city before returning to lead the League?
questions.explanation: Jinnah spent several years practising law in London before returning to revitalise the Muslim League.

questions.q: Jinnah called for 'Direct Action Day' in 1946 to press which demand?
options: Dominion status for a united India
questions.explanation: Direct Action Day in August 1946 was called to press the League's demand for Pakistan.

questions.q: In his famous 11 August 1947 address to Pakistan's Constituent Assembly, Jinnah spoke of citizens being free to go to their own places of worship regardless of what?
questions.explanation: In his 11 August 1947 speech Jinnah said people of every religion, caste, or creed would be free and equal citizens of the state.

questions.q: Which constitutional theory did Jinnah become the foremost spokesman for in the 1940s?
questions.explanation: Jinnah became the leading exponent of the two-nation theory, that Hindus and Muslims were distinct nations.

questions.q: Jinnah's only daughter, Dina, married a man of which background, a marriage that strained their relationship?
questions.explanation: Jinnah's daughter Dina married Neville Wadia, of a Parsi business family, which estranged her from her father.

questions.q: Jinnah's own wife, Rattanbai 'Ruttie' Petit, came from which prominent community?
questions.explanation: Jinnah married Rattanbai Petit, who came from a wealthy Parsi family.

questions.q: Jinnah served for years as a member of which colonial legislative body, representing Bombay Muslims?
questions.explanation: Jinnah sat in the Imperial Legislative Council as an elected member.

questions.q: Which Congress leader famously described Jinnah, in earlier years, as the best ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu is widely credited with hailing Jinnah as an ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity.

questions.q: In which year did Muhammad Ali Jinnah die, shortly after Pakistan's creation?
questions.explanation: Jinnah died in 1948, about a year after Pakistan's founding.

questions.q: Jinnah began his legal training at which Inn of Court in London?
questions.explanation: Jinnah was called to the bar from Lincoln's Inn in London.

questions.q: Jinnah resigned from the Congress in 1920 over the launch of which movement, deepening his break with Gandhi?
questions.explanation: Jinnah parted ways with the Congress over the launch of the Non-Cooperation Movement around 1920.

questions.q: The 1944 talks between Jinnah and Gandhi, which ended without agreement, are commonly referred to by what name?
questions.explanation: The 1944 Gandhi-Jinnah talks in Bombay failed to reconcile the Congress and League positions.

questions.q: Jinnah's ill health in his final years was due chiefly to which disease, kept largely secret from the public?
questions.explanation: Jinnah suffered from tuberculosis, a fact concealed during the critical Partition negotiations.

questions.q: Which 1937 election outcome is often cited as a turning point that hardened Jinnah's stance after the Congress declined to share power with the League?
options: The Congress boycott of all provinces
Congress refusal of a coalition in the United Provinces
questions.explanation: The Congress's refusal to form a coalition with the League in the United Provinces after 1937 is often seen as hardening Jinnah's separatist resolve.

questions.q: Jinnah is often referred to in Pakistan by an additional honorific besides Quaid-e-Azam, calling him the 'Father of' the nation. What is that title?
questions.explanation: Jinnah is also honoured in Pakistan as Baba-e-Qaum, the Father of the Nation.

questions.q: In what month and year did Jinnah preside over the League session at Lahore that adopted the resolution for separate states?
questions.explanation: The Lahore session at which the resolution was adopted was held in March 1940.
